title: Australia Enters New Energy Future with First Public Hydrogen Station at Viva Energy Hub in Geelong
content:
Viva Energy launched its Australia-first renewable hydrogen refuelling station at the company's Energy Hub in Geelong. The facility is the nationâ€™s first publicly accessible commercial hydrogen refuelling station and will be the hub for a fleet of hydrogen-powered, emission-free commercial heavy vehicles servicing the Geelong region.

Todayâ€™s launch, which was attended by Deputy Prime Minister Richard Marles and the Victorian Minister for Climate Action, Energy and Resources Lily Dâ€™Ambrosio, marks a significant step in Viva Energyâ€™s mission to support its customers and partners in the transition to cleaner energy solutions, reinforcing the companyâ€™s commitment to sustainability and innovation.

The project incorporates a 2.5-megawatt electrolyser for the generation of renewable hydrogen using recycled water from Barwon Waterâ€™s Northern Water Plant. The project includes a fleet of hydrogen fuel cell electric vehicles (FCEVs) â€“ including prime movers, buses and rubbish trucks â€“ operated by local businesses which will utilise the Viva Energy Hub for refuelling.
